expectations.text: A .md file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ — confirming the skill triggered without an explicit /capture-insight slash command
expectations.evidence: File confirmed at /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ai_engineering/2026-03-19_chunking-beats-embeddings-domain-corpora.md. The run_summary.txt confirms it was written there. The eval prompt is a casual conversation message with no slash command.

expectations.text: The file contains all four sections: ## The Struggle, ## The Drill-Down, ## The Aha Moment, ## The One-Liner
expectations.evidence: All four sections present in the actual file at lines 10, 14, 18, and 22 respectively: '## The Struggle', '## The Drill-Down', '## The Aha Moment', '## The One-Liner'.
Line 5 of the file: '**Status:** raw'. The field name and value both match.

expectations.text: The file content references '28%' — this specific detail from the prompt must be preserved
expectations.evidence: Found twice in the file: in The Drill-Down ('the scores jumped 28%') and in The One-Liner ('RAGAS jumped 28%'). The specific figure is preserved verbatim.

expectations.text: The Struggle and Drill-Down sections use the user's phrasing — 'barely moved the needle' should appear rather than a formal paraphrase
expectations.evidence: Exact phrase 'barely moved the needle' appears in The Struggle section: 'I spent two days swapping embedding models and barely moved the needle.' It also appears in The One-Liner. No formal paraphrase was substituted.

expectations.text: The Aha Moment correctly captures that chunk boundaries (not model size) were the root cause of retrieval failures
expectations.evidence: The Aha Moment reads: 'If your chunks are cutting across paragraph boundaries, you're feeding the embedding model broken context — and no embedding model, no matter how good, can recover meaning from a fragment that lost its beginning or end.' This explicitly identifies chunk boundaries as root cause and explicitly rules out model size as the lever.

execution_metrics.note: No metrics.json found in outputs dir. Estimates derived from run_summary.txt and timing.json.

claims.claim: Skill triggered automatically from a casual conversation message without an explicit slash command
claims.evidence: The eval prompt is a conversational message ('Two days on embeddings...') with no slash command. The run_summary.txt confirms the file was created. The transcript shows the skill fired from that casual input.

claims.claim: User's exact phrasing was preserved: 'barely moved the needle', 'jumped 28%', 'don't skip it'
claims.evidence: All three phrases verified in the actual file: 'barely moved the needle' (The Struggle), 'scores jumped 28%' (The Drill-Down), 'RAGAS jumped 28%' and 'don't skip it' (The One-Liner).

claims.claim: INDEX.md was updated with the new entry
claims.evidence: outputs/INDEX.md contains a row for '2026-03-19_chunking-beats-embeddings-domain-corpora.md' with status 'raw'. run_summary.txt confirms INDEX.md was updated (not created) and that a concurrent-write conflict was detected and handled by re-reading before writing.

claims.claim: No errors were encountered during execution
claims.evidence: run_summary.txt ERRORS section reads: 'None. All files written successfully.'

workarounds: INDEX.md had a concurrent write conflict — a new entry was added between the initial read and first write attempt. The executor re-read INDEX.md before writing to preserve the new entry. Final INDEX.md is correct.

suggestions.assertion: A .md file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ — confirming the skill triggered without an explicit /capture-insight slash command
suggestions.reason: The assertion conflates two things: file existence and trigger mechanism. A skill that required a slash command but still produced the file would pass this assertion. Consider splitting: one assertion for file existence, one that explicitly checks the eval prompt contains no slash command and the skill still fired.

No assertion checks the filename format. The skill produced '2026-03-19_chunking-beats-embeddings-domain-corpora.md' — a correctly slugified, date-prefixed filename. A skill that wrote the file with a random or wrong name would pass all six assertions. Adding a filename pattern check (date prefix + slug derived from topic) would catch that failure mode.

No assertion checks that the One-Liner is a single sentence or short pithy statement rather than a paragraph. The One-Liner section is defined as a compact distillation — a multi-sentence rambling output would satisfy the current assertion ('section exists') but violate the format intent.

eval_feedback.overall: All six assertions pass with strong, specific evidence. The main gap is that the trigger-mechanism assertion is underspecified, the filename format is unchecked, and the One-Liner format constraint has no coverage.
